desc.list: Button lists include specific design guidelines for handling vertically repeated buttons.
desc.size: Button sizes adjust the overall dimensions using padding, text size and inline icon size.
desc.block: Block‑level buttons always expand to fill the width of their parent element at every screen size.
desc.multi: Multiple buttons can wrap onto one or more lines when space is limited.
desc.style: Buttons can combine multiple styles—borders, rounded or circular shapes, and hover‑revealed backgrounds (like ghost buttons).
desc.theme: Buttons can use any theme color for their borders, backgrounds and text, and these can be combined with any style variant.
desc.holder: Holder lists include specific design guidelines for handling horizontally repeated buttons.
desc.paging: All button variations can be used for paging. On small screens, only the active, previous, and next buttons remain visible.
desc.square: Square buttons always maintain a square aspect ratio, and they can be used with any of the existing button sizes.
desc.noStyle: No‑style buttons reset the browser’s default appearance and give you a clean, neutral starting point
desc.holderRes: Button holders can adapt responsively across breakpoints.
desc.holderDrop: Dropdowns can be used inside button holders.
desc.listBorder: Button lists manage and control the borders between repeated buttons.
desc.responsive: Inline and square buttons can be responsive to any of the xs, sm, or md breakpoints.
desc.holderBorder: Holder lists manage and control the borders between repeated buttons.
desc.activePassive: Active buttons use bold text, while passive buttons disable pointer events and display reduced opacity.
